title: Mega Meganium Battle Guide
summary: A [Grass]/[Fairy] bulky special [tank] with 143 Special Attack, combining strong offensive output with self-healing and defensive utility.
strengths: 143 Special Attack makes it hit harder than most walls
115/115 dual bulk provides broad physical and special [tank]ing
weaknesses: Gains [Poison] and [Steel] weaknesses from the [Fairy] type
Still only 80 Speed — moves last against most attackers
Turn one before mega evolving still runs the base ability

recommendedMoves.reason: [Grass] STAB that heals on hit for sustain

recommendedItems.reason: Required mega stone for the Meganium mega form

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Meganium evolves into a [Grass]/[Fairy] bulky special [tank], gaining a 143 Special Attack stat and 115/115 defensive bulk to become one of the most flexible [support] [tank]s. The new typing resists [Dragon], [Dark], and [Fighting] — three of the most common offensive types in the tier. The 143 SpA means it isn't passive either; [moonblast] and [giga-drain] pressure switch-ins instead of just [absorb]ing hits.

## Strengths
The [Grass]/[Fairy] type combination is defensively excellent, offering a strong cluster of resistances that the base form can't reach. [moonblast] threatens [Dragon], [Dark], and [Fighting] attackers, [giga-drain] heals Mega Meganium while dealing damage, and [synthesis] provides a second layer of [recover]y on top of the passive drain from [giga-drain]. 115 Defense and 115 Special Defense means physical and special threats are both buffered effectively, making Mega Meganium one of the few dual-side special [tank]s in the metagame.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The [Fairy] type brings two new weaknesses. [sludge-wave], [sludge-bomb], and [flash-cannon] all threaten to break Mega Meganium's bulk faster than its sustain can [recover]. 80 Speed also means almost every oppo[sing] attacker moves first, so one prediction error is hard to [recover] from. The pre-mega turn still runs [overgrow] or leaf-guard, which can trip up the first attack if you're not careful.

## Recommended Moveset
[giga-drain] is the locked [Grass] STAB for both damage and sustain. [moonblast] is the second STAB and handles the [Dragon], [Dark], and [Fighting] attackers Mega Meganium is meant to wall. [earth-power] covers [Poison] and [Steel] walls that would otherwise resist the STAB. [synthesis] fills the fourth slot as a dedicated full-heal, though [aromatherapy] is a reasonable alternate if team-wide status removal is a bigger priority.

## Item · Ability · Tera
The item is always [meganiumite]. The ability is locked to [mega-sol] after evolution. Tera [Fairy] maxes [moonblast] damage and preserves the defensive profile, while Tera [Steel] flips the new [Poison] weakness into an immunity and covers most of the remaining holes. Tera choice depends on which attackers the team struggles against most.

## Teammates & Synergy
A [Poison] and [Steel] answer is mandatory. Speed control through [tailwind], [trick-room], or screens helps Mega Meganium land its attacks de[spite] the 80 Speed tier. A fast cleaner on the back line converts the defensive turns Mega Meganium buys into wins, and a physical wall partner creates a defensive core that covers both sides of damage.

## Play Patterns
Mega evolve as soon as Mega Meganium is safe, then run the [giga-drain] plus [synthesis] [recover]y loop to outlast whichever attacker it's walling. Use [moonblast] to break [Dragon] and [Dark] switch-ins, and [earth-power] for [Steel] and [Poison] pivots. Save [synthesis] for moments when HP drops below 50 percent, and use the Tera exclusively on a telegraphed [Poison] or [Steel] move.
